A staple among Druid builds, the
Druid harnesses the incredible AoE capabilities of the ‍
Ultimate to effortlessly clear packs of monsters.
features an unusual blend of Storm, Earth, and Werewolf tags, with the build primarily played in Werebear form. This means it has access to the best Druid multipliers, and can take advantage of
As a duration skill, overlapping your ‍‍
casts is vital to the overall smoothness of the build, and Cooldown Reduction alongside ‍‍

Druid is also easy to play, with the majority of your skills being automated, making it a great choice for speedfarming or for players who enjoy a low-maintenance build capable of tackling a wide variety of endgame activities.  This guide will teach you everything you need to know about the ‍‍
Guide updated for Season 14 / Patch 3.1.0 (Season of Death Awakening)
Guide updated for Season 13 / Patch 3.0 (Lord of Hatred)

Druid is a maelstrom of destruction, capable of almost immediately offscreening lesser foes while obtaining respectable marks in Pushing. The high gear requirements can be off-putting at first, but stick with it, and you'll be enjoying one of the most prolific speedfarming builds in Diablo 4.

Bosses are immune to Crowd Control (CC) effects and instead take Stagger Damage when hit by one. The amount of Stagger Damage dealt depends on the type of CC and is proportional to its duration. After being hit by a CC effect, the Boss temporarily takes less Stagger Damage from a CC of the same type until the duration of the CC it would have applied has run its course.

Effectively, this means that when you're just spamming one type of CC you don't need to spam any more of it than what you need to keep a normal enemy permanently CC'd. To stagger the Boss quickly you instead need to use as many different types of CC as possible.

While a Boss is Staggered, it counts as having all forms of Crowd Control applied to it simultaneously, potentially ramping up your damage significantly from situational bonuses. After the Stagger ends, they become x5 harder to Stagger again for the next 20 seconds. Each party member additionally increases the Stagger threshold by 50%.
